route: RUNTIME_KV_CORE: native cache store (cache[slice].store) + owned tile reads cache halves as AFTER-nodes (cache[0,0].after(store)); @function bypass; default-off flag
key_correction: This BYTE-IDENTICAL non-baking route CORRECTS the prior 'callify hard-stop / runtime-KV bakes' conclusion. The baking was specific to the OPAQUE CUSTOM_KERNEL append; the NATIVE store + AFTER-read does NOT bake. Runtime-KV correctness is achievable.
why_no_wd: The AFTER-read of cache SLICES (cache[0,0]) still materializes the K/V halves -- W==D identical to default (which also slices+materializes). The materialization is NOT a missing runtime-KV persistence capability; it is the owned tile READING THE CACHE VIA SLICES. The default owned route materializes the same way.
real_lever: Remove materialization by reading the cache via BUFFER IDENTITY (whole buffer, no slice) -> callify transform_precompiled_call passes a BUFFER-identity AFTER-node directly (no force-contiguous, no materialization). Requires either (a) separate K/V cache buffers (model change + one-time prefill->decode copy), or (b) a kernel V-offset so the tile reads K and V halves from the one cache buffer. Both are BOUNDED tile/cache-layout changes -- NOT a core-runtime persistence/Tensor-purity project.
